title: Late-season consequence and homecoming pass
summary: Packet 128 should cash in the success of High Pass by making the north end of the season feel more consequential and by giving station returns a little more visible payoff, without opening season three, biome six, or a broader planner shell.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the lane-1 homecoming payoff seam and station-shell hardening pass.
execution_order.why_now: The return loop needs more visible earned payoff, and overlay composition still carries one of the remaining concentration risks.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the lane-2 compact Tundra relationship-teaching pass.
execution_order.why_now: High Pass now feels chapter-grade, so the broader late-season north end should teach one clearer earlier-band ecological relationship instead of leaving Tundra as a route endpoint.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the lane-3 earlier-band Tundra remembered-place pass.
execution_order.why_now: The next best spatial spend is an earlier Tundra place the player remembers by feel, not more density in the far-right meltwater pocket.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the lane-4 stronger Thaw Window consequence pass.
execution_order.why_now: Route v2 has already proved support and replay seams; the next leverage is one route-local consequence that changes what the player notices or reaches for during the active window.

guardrails: Do not open season three, biome six, or a broader planner/dashboard shell in this packet.
Keep all new station and route payoff handheld-safe at `256x160` and prefer visual or spatial consequence over more prose.
Do not reopen nursery surgery unless a real overlap or regression reappears.
Do not spend the immediate next wave on more beach-opener or extra High Pass geometry density.
Before future external review drops, omit `node_modules` and verify a clean install with `npm install`, `npm run build`, `npm test`, and `npm run validate:agents`.

main_315_focus.chapter_shell_target: Extract the existing field-station shell accent family out of `overlay-render.ts`, then use that same shell family for one late-season archived-return payoff instead of adding more copy or another panel.
main_315_focus.protective_split_target: Split the current shell accent cluster (`resolveFieldStationGrowthAccentState`, `resolveFieldStationBackdropAccentState`, `resolveFieldStationBackdropPulseState`, plus the paired draw helpers) into a dedicated renderer helper such as `src/engine/field-station-homecoming-shell.ts` so late-season return logic does not regrow inline inside `overlay-render.ts`.
verification_targets: The default station shell stays unchanged when no meaningful return state is active.
A mid-progression return reuses the current brace family without adding another card or row.
A late-season archived return reads as more earned through one calm upper-shell lintel on the same brace family.
non_goals: Do not add a new station tab.
Do not add an expedition dashboard or planner row.
Do not reopen nursery layout work unless a regression appears.
Do not add new subtitle or station-note copy just to explain the payoff.

main_316_focus.chapter_target: Retune the existing `between-tussocks` thaw-skirt note so the late-season north end teaches one clearer thaw-hold relationship through channel, sedge, and willow instead of adding a ninth tundra note.
preferred_surfaces: ecosystem-note fallback prompt only unless runtime proof shows a dedicated seed is necessary

exact_note_target.summary_direction: Teach that sedge and willow hold the edges while wetter thaw channels stay low between them.
exact_note_target.prompt_direction: Use one compact ecosystem-note fallback question about what keeps thaw water low and slow.

non_goals: Do not add another comparison shell or archive panel.
Do not widen the notebook or journal shell.
Do not crowd the far-right meltwater rest with more copy.
Do not add a brand-new tundra note id unless the refreshed existing seam proves impossible to surface cleanly.

main_317_focus.chapter_target: Add one remembered Tundra place in the earlier `snow-meadow -> thaw-skirt` transition so the north end reads as more place-based before the far-right meltwater pocket.
main_317_focus.preferred_zone: thaw-skirt upper shelf into bank shoulder
preferred_space: one compact thaw bench or wet-bank rest inside the existing thaw-skirt shelf family
at most one tiny return step if the bench needs a calmer handoff into frost ridge
non_goals: Do not spend more density on the existing `meltwater-bank-rest` pocket.
Do not reopen the `snow-meadow` drift-hold strip except for tiny spacing fallout from the thaw-band change.
Do not add a new climb family, trap geometry, or corridor rewrite.
Do not widen the chapter into another High Pass-style geometry push.

main_318_focus.chapter_target: Deepen `Thaw Window` with one route-local consequence that changes what the player notices or reaches for during the active window while keeping the station, notebook, and filed identity stable.

preferred_shape.title: Thaw Window
preferred_shape.constraint: Alter the in-field clue or approach feel during the active window without opening a second tundra route.

preferred_proof_band.zone: thaw-skirt upper shelf and channel shoulder

replay_target.priority: Spend the next route-local consequence on the middle wet-tuft leg instead of repeating the first-bloom proof.

main_318_focus.support_target: Reuse the existing support-choice model so the player can feel a meaningful difference during the active window, but do not add another support row, HUD chrome, or broader replay framework.
non_goals: Do not add a second Tundra route.
Do not widen the station or notebook shell.
Do not rename the route without a real play-feel difference.
